description: Claude persistent memory via iCloud — knowledge graph + deep context archive
user_description: Gives Claude a persistent memory so it remembers your preferences, projects, and past conversations across sessions and devices. Optional family memory lets you share shared context (insurance, house, pets, vehicles) with your partner across their separate Claude install.
usage_instructions: Memory works automatically once installed. Claude will remember things you tell it between conversations — your preferences, project details, and important context. If you enable family memory during setup, shared facts sync via iCloud between family members. You don't need to do anything special — just say "remember this" in conversation.

included_servers.name: Knowledge Graph
included_servers.description: Stores structured facts, people, projects, and relationships as quick-access memory

included_servers.name: Deep Context
included_servers.description: Stores long-form session summaries, creative briefs, and decision logs

steps.title: What's Your First Name?
steps.body: This name will label your memory servers in Claude — for example, 'Tiera-Memory' and 'Tiera-Deep-Context'. Each family member gets their own name.

validation.error_message: Letters, numbers, spaces, and dashes only

steps.title: Enable Family Memory?
steps.body: Family memory is a shared space in iCloud where you and your partner can store facts you both need — insurance plans, house details, pet info, vehicle records. Only one person per household needs to enable it; the other family member will see it automatically once iCloud syncs.

options.label: Yes, set up family memory
options.description: Creates the shared iCloud folder and adds routing to Claude

options.label: Skip — personal memory only
options.description: You can enable family memory later from the terminal

steps.title: Ready to Install
steps.body:
Claude will get two memory servers:

• {{user_input:DISPLAY_NAME}}-Memory — quick-access knowledge graph
• {{user_input:DISPLAY_NAME}}-Deep-Context — session summaries and decision logs

Your memory is stored in iCloud and syncs across devices. If you enabled family memory, the shared family folder will be set up too.

user_description: Lets Claude read, create, and manage your Apple Reminders. Claude can add tasks to your lists, check off completed items, and help you stay organized.
usage_instructions: Just ask Claude about your reminders naturally — "What's on my grocery list?", "Add milk to my shopping list", or "Mark the dentist appointment as done." Claude can see all your Reminders lists and manage them for you.

description: iMessage read/send integration via SQLite + AppleScript
user_description: Lets Claude read and send iMessages on your behalf. Claude can check your recent messages, search conversations, and send texts to your contacts.
usage_instructions: Ask Claude things like "What did Mom text me?", "Send a message to John saying I'll be late", or "Search my messages for the restaurant name." Claude can read your message history and send new messages through iMessage.

setup_flow.title: Set Up iMessage
setup_flow.subtitle: Grant Claude access to your Messages

steps.title: Grant Full Disk Access to Claude
steps.body:
macOS protects your iMessages with Full Disk Access. Claude needs this permission to read your message history.

Click Open below to jump straight to the right pane in System Settings (Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access). Then click +, navigate to /Applications/Claude.app, and toggle it on.

Look for Full Disk Access — not Screen & System Audio Recording. They're easy to confuse.

steps.title: Ready to Install
steps.body:
Fort Abode will install the iMessage MCP and configure Claude. After install, fully quit Claude Desktop with ⌘Q and reopen so it picks up the new permission.

You can re-check Full Disk Access anytime from the iMessage tile — Fort Abode will warn you if it gets revoked by a future macOS or Claude update.

description: Connect any Notion workspace to Claude via API integration
user_description: Lets Claude read and write pages, databases, and comments in a Notion workspace. Add multiple workspaces — one for each person or team.
usage_instructions: Tell Claude which workspace to use by name. Example: 'Search Tiera's Notion for upcoming shows' or 'Update my project page in Notion.'

setup_flow.title: Add Notion Workspace
setup_flow.subtitle: Connect a Notion workspace to Claude

steps.title: Sign In to the Right Account
steps.body: Important: Log into the Notion account that owns the workspace you want to connect — not your own account (unless it's your workspace). Then click the button below to open the integrations page.

steps.title: Create New Integration
steps.body: Click 'New integration', name it 'Claude Access', select the workspace, and click Submit. If the workspace owner already created a 'Claude Access' integration, skip this step and use that one.

steps.title: Paste the Token
steps.body: On the integration page, click 'Show' next to Internal Integration Secret, then copy and paste it below. This token comes from the workspace owner's account.

validation.error_message: Token should start with ntn_ or secret_

steps.title: Share Pages with Claude
steps.body: Still in the workspace owner's Notion, open each page or database you want Claude to access. Click ••• → Add connections → select 'Claude Access'. Without this step, Claude can't see anything.

steps.title: Name This Workspace
steps.body: Give this workspace a short name so you can tell Claude which one to use (e.g., 'Tiera' or 'Band').

validation.error_message: Letters, numbers, spaces, and dashes only

steps.title: Ready to Install
steps.body: Claude will be able to access the {{user_input:WORKSPACE_NAME}} Notion workspace. You can tell Claude 'check {{user_input:WORKSPACE_NAME}}'s Notion' and it'll know which workspace to use.

description: Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Sheets, Docs, and more via Google's official API
user_description: Lets Claude read and send Gmail, manage Google Calendar events, access Google Drive files, edit Sheets and Docs, and more.
usage_instructions:
After installing, copy and paste this to Claude to add your first Google account:

"Use the Google Workspace MCP manage_accounts tool to add my Google account."

The MCP will open your browser for Google sign-in. Once connected, use natural language: 'Check my Gmail', 'Add a meeting Friday at 2pm', 'Find the budget spreadsheet in Drive'. To add more accounts, ask Claude to run manage_accounts again.

description: Weekly planning dashboard — unified work + life rhythm
user_description: Plans your entire week in one dashboard. Synthesizes calendars, reminders, email, and Notion into a clean weekly rhythm with time blocks, triage, and priorities.
usage_instructions: Run '/weekly-rhythm-engine' in Claude Code on Fridays to plan the coming week, or anytime something changes. Your dashboard opens in the browser automatically.

steps.title: What's Your First Name?
steps.body: Each family member gets their own config folder. Your weekly preferences will be saved under your name.

steps.title: Ready to Install
steps.body:
Fort Abode will set up the Weekly Rhythm Engine in iCloud:

• engine-spec.md — the planning engine (auto-updated)
• dashboard-template.html — the dashboard layout (auto-updated)
• {{user_input:DISPLAY_NAME}}/config.md — your personal config (created on first run)

Run '/weekly-rhythm-engine' in Claude Code to generate your first dashboard.

description: Traffic-aware travel itinerary skill for Claude Desktop
user_description: Builds accurate travel itineraries from boarding time backwards — every drive leg uses live traffic-aware data from Google Maps. Plans dog drop-offs, rental car returns, and gas stops, then saves the result to Apple Notes. Powers Travel Mode in the Weekly Rhythm Engine.
usage_instructions: Once installed, mention a flight, boarding time, or trip in any Claude Desktop conversation — the skill auto-triggers. Try “I fly out at 6am, plan my trip to BNA.” The skill loads automatically when relevant.

setup_flow.title: Install Travel Itinerary
setup_flow.subtitle: Download the .mcpb bundle and let Claude Desktop finish the install

steps.title: How This Install Works
steps.body:
Travel Itinerary ships as a self-contained Claude Desktop extension (.mcpb).

1. Fort Abode downloads the latest .mcpb from GitHub
2. macOS opens it in Claude Desktop
3. Claude Desktop prompts you for your Google Maps API key (stored in its own encrypted store)
4. You’re done — the skill is registered and auto-triggers on any travel question

You’ll need a Google Maps API key with Directions API + Geocoding API enabled. If you don’t have one yet, create it before continuing.

steps.title: Ready to Install
steps.body:
Fort Abode will download travel-itinerary-1.0.0.mcpb from GitHub and hand it to Claude Desktop. Claude Desktop will pop up an install dialog and ask for your Google Maps API key.

If Claude Desktop isn’t already running, it’ll launch automatically.
